description_long: This object is a large, cubic stone block with a grid pattern of deep, dark lines dividing its surface into smaller square sections. The surface texture is rough and slightly uneven, resembling concrete or stone, with a matte finish that diffuses light. The grid lines are consistent in width and depth, giving the block a modern, modular appearance often seen in urban environments. The coloration is predominantly a light gray or off-white, with the grid lines appearing much darker, almost black. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering, suggesting it is relatively new or well-maintained. The block is solid and robust, likely used as a barrier or obstacle in public spaces such as sidewalks, plazas, or parks.
description: A large, cubic stone block with a rough, light gray surface divided by deep, dark grid lines, giving it a modern, modular look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the grid pattern clearly, with evenly spaced dark lines on a light, rough surface.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, maintaining the same grid pattern and surface texture.
description_view_2: Another rotated view, still showing the uniform grid and rough stone texture without visible variation.
description_view_3: Final view continues the consistent grid and texture, with no distinguishing marks or features.
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering or erosion; surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous modular barrier or wall
